1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: MPL-2.0
3: 
4: package bsr
5: 
6: import (
7: 	"bytes"
8: 	"context"
9: 	"encoding/json"
10: 	"errors"
11: 	"fmt"
12: 	"io"
13: 
14: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/bsr/internal/checksum"
15: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/bsr/internal/is"
16: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/bsr/internal/journal"
17: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/bsr/internal/sign"
18: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/bsr/kms"
19: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/storage"
20: 	wrapping "github.com/hashicorp/go-kms-wrapping/v2"
21: 	"github.com/hashicorp/go-kms-wrapping/v2/extras/crypto"
22: 	"google.golang.org/protobuf/proto"
23: )
24: 
25: const (
26: 	metaFile     = "%s-recording.meta"
27: 	summaryFile  = "%s-recording-summary.json"
28: 	checksumFile = "SHA256SUM"
29: 	sigFile      = "SHA256SUM.sig"
30: 	journalFile  = ".journal"
31: )
32: 
33: // ContainerType defines the type of container.
34: type containerType string
35: 
36: // Valid container types.
37: const (
38: 	sessionContainer    containerType = "session"
39: 	connectionContainer containerType = "connection"
40: 	channelContainer    containerType = "channel"
41: )
42: 
43: // container contains a group of files in a BSR.
44: // Each container has corresponding .meta, .summary, SHA256SUM, and SHA256SUM.sig files.
45: type container struct {
46: 	container storage.Container
47: 
48: 	// Fields primarily used for writing
49: 	journal    *journal.Journal
50: 	sumName    string
51: 	meta       *checksum.File
52: 	sum        *checksum.File
53: 	sumEncoder *json.Encoder
54: 	checksums  *sign.File
55: 	sigs       storage.File
56: 
57: 	// Field used for reading
58: 	shaSums  checksum.Sha256Sums
59: 	metaFile storage.File
60: 
61: 	// Field used for reading and writing
62: 	keys     *kms.Keys
63: 	metaName string
64: }
65: 
66: // newContainer creates a container for the given type backed by the provide storage.Container.
67: func newContainer(ctx context.Context, t containerType, c storage.Container, keys *kms.Keys) (*container, error) {
68: 	j, err := c.OpenFile(ctx, journalFile,
69: 		storage.WithCreateFile(),
70: 		storage.WithFileAccessMode(storage.WriteOnly),
71: 		storage.WithCloseSyncMode(storage.NoSync))
72: 	if err != nil {
73: 		return nil, err
74: 	}
75: 	jj, err := journal.New(ctx, j)
76: 	if err != nil {
77: 		return nil, err
78: 	}
79: 	cc := &container{
80: 		container: c,
81: 		journal:   jj,
82: 		keys:      keys,
83: 	}
84: 
85: 	cc.sigs, err = cc.create(ctx, sigFile)
86: 	if err != nil {
87: 		return nil, err
88: 	}
89: 
90: 	cs, err := cc.create(ctx, checksumFile)
91: 	if err != nil {
92: 		return nil, err
93: 	}
94: 	cc.checksums, err = sign.NewFile(ctx, cs, cc.sigs, keys)
95: 	if err != nil {
96: 		return nil, err
97: 	}
98: 
99: 	cc.metaName = fmt.Sprintf(metaFile, t)
100: 	meta, err := cc.create(ctx, cc.metaName)
101: 	if err != nil {
102: 		return nil, err
103: 	}
104: 	cc.meta, err = checksum.NewFile(ctx, meta, cc.checksums)
105: 	if err != nil {
106: 		return nil, err
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	cc.sumName = fmt.Sprintf(summaryFile, t)
110: 	sum, err := cc.create(ctx, cc.sumName)
111: 	if err != nil {
112: 		return nil, err
113: 	}
114: 	cc.sum, err = checksum.NewFile(ctx, sum, cc.checksums)
115: 	if err != nil {
116: 		return nil, err
117: 	}
118: 	cc.sumEncoder = json.NewEncoder(cc.sum)
119: 	cc.sumEncoder.SetIndent("", "  ")
120: 
121: 	return cc, nil
122: }
123: 
124: type populateKeyFunc func(c *container) (*kms.Keys, error)
125: 
126: // openContainer will set keys and load and verify the checksums for this container
127: func openContainer(ctx context.Context, t containerType, c storage.Container, keyGetFunc populateKeyFunc) (*container, error) {
128: 	const op = "bsr.openContainer"
129: 	switch {
130: 	case t == "":
131: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing container type: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
132: 	case is.Nil(c):
133: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing container: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
134: 	case is.Nil(keyGetFunc):
135: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing key function: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
136: 	}
137: 
138: 	cc := &container{
139: 		container: c,
140: 	}
141: 
142: 	keys, err := keyGetFunc(cc)
143: 	if err != nil {
144: 		return nil, err
145: 	}
146: 	cc.keys = keys
147: 
148: 	err = cc.loadChecksums(ctx)
149: 	if err != nil {
150: 		return nil, err
151: 	}
152: 
153: 	// Load the meta file
154: 	cc.metaName = fmt.Sprintf(metaFile, t)
155: 	mFile, err := cc.container.OpenFile(ctx, cc.metaName)
156: 	if err != nil {
157: 		return nil, err
158: 	}
159: 	cc.metaFile = mFile
160: 
161: 	return cc, nil
162: }
163: 
164: func (c *container) loadChecksums(ctx context.Context) error {
165: 	const op = "bsr.(container).loadChecksums"
166: 
167: 	// Open and extract checksum signature
168: 	checksumsSigFile, err := c.container.OpenFile(ctx, sigFile)
169: 	if err != nil {
170: 		return err
171: 	}
172: 	checksumSigBytes := new(bytes.Buffer)
173: 	_, err = checksumSigBytes.ReadFrom(checksumsSigFile)
174: 	if err != nil {
175: 		return err
176: 	}
177: 
178: 	sig := new(wrapping.SigInfo)
179: 	err = proto.Unmarshal(checksumSigBytes.Bytes(), sig)
180: 	if err != nil {
181: 		return err
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	// Open and extract checksum file bytes
185: 	checksumsFile, err := c.container.OpenFile(ctx, checksumFile)
186: 	if err != nil {
187: 		return err
188: 	}
189: 	var checksumsBuffer bytes.Buffer
190: 	cTee := io.TeeReader(checksumsFile, &checksumsBuffer)
191: 
192: 	checksumBytes := new(bytes.Buffer)
193: 	_, err = checksumBytes.ReadFrom(cTee)
194: 	if err != nil {
195: 		return err
196: 	}
197: 
198: 	verified, err := c.keys.VerifySignatureWithPubKey(ctx, sig, checksumBytes.Bytes())
199: 	if err != nil {
200: 		return err
201: 	}
202: 	if !verified {
203: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: failed to verify checksums signature: %w", op, ErrSignatureVerification)
204: 	}
205: 
206: 	// Load checksums
207: 	sums, err := checksum.LoadSha256Sums(&checksumsBuffer)
208: 	if err != nil {
209: 		return err
210: 	}
211: 	c.shaSums = sums
212: 
213: 	return nil
214: }
215: 
216: func (c *container) loadKey(ctx context.Context, keyFileName string) (*wrapping.KeyInfo, error) {
217: 	keyFile, err := c.container.OpenFile(ctx, keyFileName)
218: 	if err != nil {
219: 		return nil, err
220: 	}
221: 
222: 	keyBytes := new(bytes.Buffer)
223: 	_, err = keyBytes.ReadFrom(keyFile)
224: 	if err != nil {
225: 		return nil, err
226: 	}
227: 
228: 	key := new(wrapping.KeyInfo)
229: 	err = proto.Unmarshal(keyBytes.Bytes(), key)
230: 	if err != nil {
231: 		return nil, err
232: 	}
233: 
234: 	return key, nil
235: }
236: 
237: func (c *container) loadSignature(ctx context.Context, sigFileName string) (*wrapping.SigInfo, error) {
238: 	sigFile, err := c.container.OpenFile(ctx, sigFileName)
239: 	if err != nil {
240: 		return nil, err
241: 	}
242: 
243: 	sigBytes := new(bytes.Buffer)
244: 	_, err = sigBytes.ReadFrom(sigFile)
245: 	if err != nil {
246: 		return nil, err
247: 	}
248: 
249: 	signature := new(wrapping.SigInfo)
250: 	err = proto.Unmarshal(sigBytes.Bytes(), signature)
251: 	if err != nil {
252: 		return nil, err
253: 	}
254: 
255: 	return signature, nil
256: }
257: 
258: // loadKeys will load the BSR keys from storage, unmarshal and unwrap them.
259: // After unwrapping, it will verify the key signature files before setting the keys
260: // on the container
261: func (c *container) loadKeys(ctx context.Context, keyUnwrapFn kms.KeyUnwrapCallbackFunc) (*kms.Keys, error) {
262: 	const op = "bsr.(container).loadKeys"
263: 
264: 	switch {
265: 	case keyUnwrapFn == nil:
266: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing key unwrap function: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
267: 	}
268: 
269: 	bsrPubKey, err := c.loadKey(ctx, bsrPubKeyFile)
270: 	if err != nil {
271: 		return nil, err
272: 	}
273: 
274: 	wrappedBsrKey, err := c.loadKey(ctx, wrappedBsrKeyFile)
275: 	if err != nil {
276: 		return nil, err
277: 	}
278: 
279: 	wrappedPrivKey, err := c.loadKey(ctx, wrappedPrivKeyFile)
280: 	if err != nil {
281: 		return nil, err
282: 	}
283: 
284: 	pubKeyBsrSignature, err := c.loadSignature(ctx, pubKeyBsrSignatureFile)
285: 	if err != nil {
286: 		return nil, err
287: 	}
288: 
289: 	pubKeySelfSignature, err := c.loadSignature(ctx, pubKeySelfSignatureFile)
290: 	if err != nil {
291: 		return nil, err
292: 	}
293: 
294: 	unwrappedKeys, err := keyUnwrapFn(kms.WrappedKeys{
295: 		WrappedBsrKey:  wrappedBsrKey,
296: 		WrappedPrivKey: wrappedPrivKey,
297: 	})
298: 	if err != nil {
299: 		return nil, err
300: 	}
301: 
302: 	keys := &kms.Keys{
303: 		PubKey:              bsrPubKey,
304: 		BsrKey:              unwrappedKeys.BsrKey,
305: 		PrivKey:             unwrappedKeys.PrivKey,
306: 		PubKeyBsrSignature:  pubKeyBsrSignature,
307: 		PubKeySelfSignature: pubKeySelfSignature,
308: 	}
309: 
310: 	verified, err := keys.VerifyPubKeySelfSignature(ctx)
311: 	if err != nil {
312: 		return nil, err
313: 	}
314: 	if !verified {
315: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: failed to verify public self signed key: %w", op, ErrSignatureVerification)
316: 	}
317: 
318: 	verified, err = keys.VerifyPubKeyBsrSignature(ctx)
319: 	if err != nil {
320: 		return nil, err
321: 	}
322: 	if !verified {
323: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: failed to verify pub key signature: %w", op, ErrSignatureVerification)
324: 	}
325: 
326: 	return keys, nil
327: }
328: 
329: func (c *container) verifyMetadata(ctx context.Context, sha256Reader *crypto.Sha256SumReader) error {
330: 	const op = "bsr.(container).verifyMetadata"
331: 	metaSum, err := sha256Reader.Sum(ctx, crypto.WithHexEncoding(true))
332: 	if err != nil {
333: 		return err
334: 	}
335: 	expectedMetaSum, err := c.shaSums.Sum(c.metaName)
336: 	if err != nil {
337: 		return err
338: 	}
339: 	if !bytes.Equal(expectedMetaSum, metaSum) {
340: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: meta checksum did not match expected value", op)
341: 	}
342: 	return nil
343: }
344: 
345: // create creates a new file in the container for writing.
346: func (c *container) create(ctx context.Context, s string, options ...storage.Option) (storage.File, error) {
347: 	const op = "bsr.(container).create"
348: 
349: 	err := c.journal.Record("CREATING", s)
350: 	if err != nil {
351: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
352: 	}
353: 
354: 	var f storage.File
355: 	switch len(options) {
356: 	case 0:
357: 		f, err = c.container.Create(ctx, s)
358: 		if err != nil {
359: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
360: 		}
361: 	default:
362: 		f, err = c.container.OpenFile(ctx, s, options...)
363: 		if err != nil {
364: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
365: 		}
366: 	}
367: 
368: 	jf, err := journal.NewFile(ctx, f, c.journal)
369: 	if err != nil {
370: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
371: 	}
372: 	defer c.journal.Record("CREATED", s)
373: 	return jf, nil
374: }
375: 
376: func (c *container) decodeJsonFile(ctx context.Context, s string, v any) error {
377: 	const op = "bsr.(container).decodeJsonFile"
378: 
379: 	return c.decodeFile(ctx, s, func(_ context.Context, r io.Reader) error {
380: 		dec := json.NewDecoder(r)
381: 		if err := dec.Decode(v); err != nil {
382: 			return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
383: 		}
384: 		return nil
385: 	})
386: }
387: 
388: func (c *container) decodeFile(ctx context.Context, s string, fn func(context.Context, io.Reader) error) error {
389: 	const op = "bsr.(container).decodeFile"
390: 
391: 	expectedSum, err := c.shaSums.Sum(s)
392: 	if err != nil {
393: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
394: 	}
395: 
396: 	f, err := c.container.OpenFile(ctx, s)
397: 	if err != nil {
398: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
399: 	}
400: 	sha256Reader, err := crypto.NewSha256SumReader(ctx, f)
401: 	if err != nil {
402: 		f.Close()
403: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
404: 	}
405: 	defer sha256Reader.Close()
406: 
407: 	if err := fn(ctx, sha256Reader); err != nil {
408: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
409: 	}
410: 
411: 	gotSum, err := sha256Reader.Sum(ctx, crypto.WithHexEncoding(true))
412: 	if err != nil {
413: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
414: 	}
415: 
416: 	if !bytes.Equal(expectedSum, gotSum) {
417: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: checksum did not match expected value", op)
418: 	}
419: 
420: 	return nil
421: }
422: 
423: // syncBsrKey will take the marshalled bytes of a key, write its contents to a local file,
424: // and then close it. The key file is created using the synchronous storage option, so
425: // close will block until the file is synced to remote storage
426: func (c *container) syncBsrKey(ctx context.Context, s string, data []byte) error {
427: 	const op = "bsr.(container).syncBsrKey"
428: 	switch {
429: 	case len(s) == 0:
430: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: missing file name %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
431: 	case data == nil:
432: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: missing data payload %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
433: 	}
434: 
435: 	jf, err := c.create(ctx, s, storage.WithCreateFile(),
436: 		storage.WithFileAccessMode(storage.WriteOnly),
437: 		storage.WithCloseSyncMode(storage.Synchronous))
438: 	if err != nil {
439: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
440: 	}
441: 
442: 	cf, err := checksum.NewFile(ctx, jf, c.checksums)
443: 	if err != nil {
444: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
445: 	}
446: 
447: 	_, err = cf.Write(data)
448: 	if err != nil {
449: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
450: 	}
451: 
452: 	err = cf.Close()
453: 	if err != nil {
454: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
455: 	}
456: 
457: 	return nil
458: }
459: 
460: // writeMetaString writes a string to the containers meta file.
461: func (c *container) writeMetaString(_ context.Context, s string) (int, error) {
462: 	return c.meta.WriteString(s)
463: }
464: 
465: // writeMetaLine writes a new line terminated line to the container's meta file.
466: func (c *container) writeMetaLine(_ context.Context, s string) (int, error) {
467: 	return c.meta.WriteString(s + "\n")
468: }
469: 
470: // WriteMeta writes a new line terminated key : value pair to the container's meta file
471: func (c *container) WriteMeta(_ context.Context, k, v string) (int, error) {
472: 	return c.meta.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s\n", k, v))
473: }
474: 
475: // EncodeSummary writes a new line terminated key : value pair to the container's summary file
476: func (c *container) EncodeSummary(_ context.Context, s any) error {
477: 	return c.sumEncoder.Encode(s)
478: }
479: 
480: // WriteBinaryChecksum writes a checksum for a binary file to the checksum file.
481: func (c *container) WriteBinaryChecksum(_ context.Context, sum []byte, fname string) (int, error) {
482: 	return c.checksums.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%x *%s\n", sum, fname))
483: }
484: 
485: // close closes a container, closing the underlying files in a container.
486: func (c *container) close(_ context.Context) error {
487: 	const op = "bsr.(container).close"
488: 
489: 	var closeError error
490: 
491: 	if err := c.meta.Close(); err != nil {
492: 		closeError = errors.Join(closeError, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err))
493: 	}
494: 
495: 	if err := c.sum.Close(); err != nil {
496: 		closeError = errors.Join(closeError, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err))
497: 	}
498: 
499: 	if err := c.checksums.Close(); err != nil {
500: 		closeError = errors.Join(closeError, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err))
501: 	}
502: 
503: 	if err := c.sigs.Close(); err != nil {
504: 		closeError = errors.Join(closeError, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err))
505: 	}
506: 
507: 	if err := c.journal.Close(); err != nil {
508: 		closeError = errors.Join(closeError, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err))
509: 	}
510: 
511: 	if err := c.container.Close(); err != nil {
512: 		closeError = errors.Join(closeError, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err))
513: 	}
514: 
515: 	return closeError
516: }

(End of file - total 516 lines)
</content>

1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: MPL-2.0
3: 
4: package bsr
5: 
6: import (
7: 	"context"
8: 	"encoding/json"
9: 	"errors"
10: 	"fmt"
11: 	"io"
12: 
13: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/bsr/internal/checksum"
14: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/bsr/internal/is"
15: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/bsr/kms"
16: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/storage"
17: 	"github.com/hashicorp/go-kms-wrapping/v2/extras/crypto"
18: 	"google.golang.org/protobuf/proto"
19: )
20: 
21: const (
22: 	bsrFile         = "%s.bsr"
23: 	connectionFile  = "%s.connection"
24: 	channelFile     = "%s.channel"
25: 	messagesFile    = "messages-%s.data"
26: 	requestsFile    = "requests-%s.data"
27: 	sessionMetaFile = "session-meta.json"
28: 
29: 	bsrPubKeyFile           = "bsrKey.pub"
30: 	wrappedBsrKeyFile       = "wrappedBsrKey"
31: 	wrappedPrivKeyFile      = "wrappedPrivKey"
32: 	pubKeyBsrSignatureFile  = "pubKeyBsrSignature.sign"
33: 	pubKeySelfSignatureFile = "pubKeySelfSignature.sign"
34: )
35: 
36: // Session is the top level container in a bsr that contains the files for
37: // a recorded session.
38: type Session struct {
39: 	*container
40: 	multiplexed bool
41: 
42: 	Meta        *SessionRecordingMeta
43: 	SessionMeta *SessionMeta
44: }
45: 
46: // NewSession creates a Session container for a given session id.
47: func NewSession(ctx context.Context, meta *SessionRecordingMeta, sessionMeta *SessionMeta, f storage.FS, keys *kms.Keys, options ...Option) (*Session, error) {
48: 	const op = "bsr.NewSession"
49: 
50: 	switch {
51: 	case is.Nil(meta):
52: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing meta: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
53: 	case is.Nil(sessionMeta):
54: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing session meta: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
55: 	case meta.Id == "":
56: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing session id: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
57: 	case is.Nil(sessionMeta.StaticHost) && is.Nil(sessionMeta.DynamicHost):
58: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing session host information: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
59: 	case !is.Nil(sessionMeta.StaticHost) && !is.Nil(sessionMeta.DynamicHost):
60: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: sessionMeta cannot contain both static and dynamic host information: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
61: 	case len(sessionMeta.StaticCredentialStore) == 0 && len(sessionMeta.VaultCredentialStore) == 0:
62: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing credential information: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
63: 	case is.Nil(sessionMeta.User):
64: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing session user: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
65: 	case is.Nil(sessionMeta.Target):
66: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing session target: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
67: 	case is.Nil(sessionMeta.Worker):
68: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing session worker: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
69: 	case is.Nil(f):
70: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing storage fs: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
71: 	case is.Nil(keys):
72: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing kms keys: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
73: 	}
74: 
75: 	opts := getOpts(options...)
76: 
77: 	c, err := f.New(ctx, fmt.Sprintf(bsrFile, meta.Id))
78: 	if err != nil {
79: 		return nil, err
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	nc, err := newContainer(ctx, sessionContainer, c, keys)
83: 	if err != nil {
84: 		return nil, err
85: 	}
86: 
87: 	// Sync keys and signatures
88: 	err = persistBsrSessionKeys(ctx, keys, nc)
89: 	if err != nil {
90: 		return nil, errors.Join(err, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, ErrBsrKeyPersistenceFailure))
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	sm, err := nc.create(ctx, sessionMetaFile)
94: 	if err != nil {
95: 		return nil, err
96: 	}
97: 	smw, err := checksum.NewFile(ctx, sm, nc.checksums)
98: 	if err != nil {
99: 		return nil, err
100: 	}
101: 	enc := json.NewEncoder(smw)
102: 	if err := enc.Encode(sessionMeta); err != nil {
103: 		smw.Close()
104: 		return nil, err
105: 	}
106: 	if err := smw.Close(); err != nil {
107: 		return nil, err
108: 	}
109: 
110: 	err = meta.writeMeta(ctx, nc)
111: 	if err != nil {
112: 		return nil, err
113: 	}
114: 
115: 	return &Session{
116: 		container:   nc,
117: 		multiplexed: opts.withSupportsMultiplex,
118: 		Meta:        meta,
119: 		SessionMeta: sessionMeta,
120: 	}, nil
121: }
122: 
123: // persistBsrSessionKeys will marshal, write, and close BSR keys locally before syncing to
124: // storage. Any error while syncing the key files should result in the termination of
125: // the session this recording is for.
126: // The key files synced are:
127: // * the BSR public key, bsrKey.pub
128: // * the wrapped BSR key, wrappedBsrKey
129: // * the wrapped private key, wrappedPrivKey
130: // * the public key BSR signature, pubKeyBsrSignature.sign
131: // * the public key self signature, pubKeySelfSignature.sign
132: func persistBsrSessionKeys(ctx context.Context, keys *kms.Keys, c *container) error {
133: 	const op = "bsr.persistBsrSessionKeys"
134: 	switch {
135: 	case keys.PubKey == nil:
136: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: missing kms pub key: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
137: 	case keys.WrappedBsrKey == nil:
138: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: missing kms wrapped BSR key: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
139: 	case keys.WrappedPrivKey == nil:
140: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: missing kms wrapped priv key: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
141: 	case keys.PubKeyBsrSignature == nil:
142: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: missing kms pub key BSR signature: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
143: 	case keys.PubKeySelfSignature == nil:
144: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: missing kms pub key self signature: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
145: 	}
146: 
147: 	keyFiles := map[string]proto.Message{
148: 		bsrPubKeyFile:           keys.PubKey,
149: 		wrappedBsrKeyFile:       keys.WrappedBsrKey,
150: 		wrappedPrivKeyFile:      keys.WrappedPrivKey,
151: 		pubKeyBsrSignatureFile:  keys.PubKeyBsrSignature,
152: 		pubKeySelfSignatureFile: keys.PubKeySelfSignature,
153: 	}
154: 	for f, k := range keyFiles {
155: 		b, err := proto.Marshal(k)
156: 		if err != nil {
157: 			return fmt.Errorf("%s: failed to marshal data for %s: %w", op, f, err)
158: 		}
159: 		err = c.syncBsrKey(ctx, f, b)
160: 		if err != nil {
161: 			return fmt.Errorf("%s: failed syncing bsr key %s: %w", op, f, err)
162: 		}
163: 	}
164: 
165: 	return nil
166: }
167: 
168: // OpenSession retrieves a BSR from storage using the sessionRecordingId and initializes it for reading.
169: // Encryption keys necessary for checking signed files will be unwrapped using the keyUnwrapFn
170: // Signature and checksum files will then be verified.
171: // Fields on the underlying container will be populated so that the returned Session can be used for BSR
172: // playback and conversion to formats such as asciinema
173: func OpenSession(ctx context.Context, sessionRecordingId string, f storage.FS, keyUnwrapFn kms.KeyUnwrapCallbackFunc) (*Session, error) {
174: 	const op = "bsr.OpenSession"
175: 	switch {
176: 	case sessionRecordingId == "":
177: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing session recording id: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
178: 	case f == nil:
179: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing storage: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
180: 	case keyUnwrapFn == nil:
181: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing key unwrap function: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	c, err := f.Open(ctx, fmt.Sprintf(bsrFile, sessionRecordingId))
185: 	if err != nil {
186: 		return nil, err
187: 	}
188: 
189: 	keyPopFn := func(c *container) (*kms.Keys, error) {
190: 		return c.loadKeys(ctx, keyUnwrapFn)
191: 	}
192: 	cc, err := openContainer(ctx, sessionContainer, c, keyPopFn)
193: 	if err != nil {
194: 		return nil, err
195: 	}
196: 
197: 	// Load and verify recording metadata
198: 	sha256Reader, err := crypto.NewSha256SumReader(ctx, cc.metaFile)
199: 	if err != nil {
200: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
201: 	}
202: 	meta, err := decodeSessionRecordingMeta(ctx, sha256Reader)
203: 	if err != nil {
204: 		return nil, err
205: 	}
206: 	err = cc.verifyMetadata(ctx, sha256Reader)
207: 	if err != nil {
208: 		return nil, err
209: 	}
210: 
211: 	sessionMeta := &SessionMeta{}
212: 	if err := cc.decodeJsonFile(ctx, sessionMetaFile, sessionMeta); err != nil {
213: 		return nil, err
214: 	}
215: 
216: 	session := &Session{
217: 		container:   cc,
218: 		Meta:        meta,
219: 		SessionMeta: sessionMeta,
220: 	}
221: 
222: 	return session, nil
223: }
224: 
225: // Close closes the Session container.
226: func (s *Session) Close(ctx context.Context) error {
227: 	return s.container.close(ctx)
228: }
229: 
230: // Connection is a container in a bsr for a specific connection in a session
231: // container. It contains the files for the recorded connection.
232: type Connection struct {
233: 	*container
234: 	multiplexed bool
235: 
236: 	Meta *ConnectionRecordingMeta
237: }
238: 
239: // NewConnection creates a Connection container for a given connection id.
240: func (s *Session) NewConnection(ctx context.Context, meta *ConnectionRecordingMeta) (*Connection, error) {
241: 	const op = "bsr.(Session).NewConnection"
242: 
243: 	switch {
244: 	case is.Nil(meta):
245: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing connection meta: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
246: 	case meta.Id == "":
247: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing connection id: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
248: 	}
249: 
250: 	name := fmt.Sprintf(connectionFile, meta.Id)
251: 	sc, err := s.container.container.SubContainer(ctx, name, storage.WithCreateFile(), storage.WithFileAccessMode(storage.WriteOnly))
252: 	if err != nil {
253: 		return nil, err
254: 	}
255: 	if _, err := s.WriteMeta(ctx, "connection", name); err != nil {
256: 		return nil, err
257: 	}
258: 
259: 	nc, err := newContainer(ctx, connectionContainer, sc, s.keys)
260: 	if err != nil {
261: 		return nil, err
262: 	}
263: 	if _, err := nc.WriteMeta(ctx, "id", meta.Id); err != nil {
264: 		return nil, err
265: 	}
266: 	return &Connection{
267: 		container:   nc,
268: 		multiplexed: s.multiplexed,
269: 		Meta:        meta,
270: 	}, nil
271: }
272: 
273: // OpenConnection will open and validate a BSR connection
274: func (s *Session) OpenConnection(ctx context.Context, connId string) (*Connection, error) {
275: 	const op = "bsr.(Session).OpenConnection"
276: 	switch {
277: 	case connId == "":
278: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing connection id: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
279: 	}
280: 
281: 	name := fmt.Sprintf(connectionFile, connId)
282: 
283: 	switch {
284: 	case !s.Meta.connections[name]:
285: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: connection id does not exist within this session: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
286: 	}
287: 
288: 	c, err := s.container.container.SubContainer(ctx, name)
289: 	if err != nil {
290: 		return nil, err
291: 	}
292: 
293: 	keyPopFn := func(c *container) (*kms.Keys, error) {
294: 		return s.keys, nil
295: 	}
296: 	cc, err := openContainer(ctx, connectionContainer, c, keyPopFn)
297: 	if err != nil {
298: 		return nil, err
299: 	}
300: 
301: 	// Load and verify connection metadata
302: 	sha256Reader, err := crypto.NewSha256SumReader(ctx, cc.metaFile)
303: 	if err != nil {
304: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
305: 	}
306: 	sm, err := decodeConnectionRecordingMeta(ctx, sha256Reader)
307: 	if err != nil {
308: 		return nil, err
309: 	}
310: 	err = cc.verifyMetadata(ctx, sha256Reader)
311: 	if err != nil {
312: 		return nil, err
313: 	}
314: 
315: 	connection := &Connection{
316: 		container: cc,
317: 		Meta:      sm,
318: 	}
319: 
320: 	return connection, nil
321: }
322: 
323: // NewChannel creates a Channel container for a given channel id.
324: func (c *Connection) NewChannel(ctx context.Context, meta *ChannelRecordingMeta) (*Channel, error) {
325: 	const op = "bsr.(Connection).NewChannel"
326: 
327: 	if !c.multiplexed {
328: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: connection cannot make channels: %w", op, ErrNotSupported)
329: 	}
330: 
331: 	switch {
332: 	case is.Nil(meta):
333: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing channel meta: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
334: 	case meta.Id == "":
335: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing channel id: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
336: 	}
337: 
338: 	name := fmt.Sprintf(channelFile, meta.Id)
339: 	sc, err := c.container.container.SubContainer(ctx, name, storage.WithCreateFile(), storage.WithFileAccessMode(storage.WriteOnly))
340: 	if err != nil {
341: 		return nil, err
342: 	}
343: 	if _, err := c.WriteMeta(ctx, "channel", name); err != nil {
344: 		return nil, err
345: 	}
346: 	nc, err := newContainer(ctx, channelContainer, sc, c.keys)
347: 	if err != nil {
348: 		return nil, err
349: 	}
350: 	if _, err := nc.WriteMeta(ctx, "id", meta.Id); err != nil {
351: 		return nil, err
352: 	}
353: 	if _, err := nc.WriteMeta(ctx, "channelType", meta.Type); err != nil {
354: 		return nil, err
355: 	}
356: 	return &Channel{
357: 		container: nc,
358: 		Meta:      meta,
359: 	}, nil
360: }
361: 
362: // OpenChannel will open and validate a BSR channel
363: func (c *Connection) OpenChannel(ctx context.Context, chanId string) (*Channel, error) {
364: 	const op = "bsr.OpenChannel"
365: 	switch {
366: 	case chanId == "":
367: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: missing channel id: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
368: 	}
369: 
370: 	name := fmt.Sprintf(channelFile, chanId)
371: 	switch {
372: 	case !c.Meta.channels[name]:
373: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: channel id does not exist within this connection: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
374: 	}
375: 
376: 	con, err := c.container.container.SubContainer(ctx, name)
377: 	if err != nil {
378: 		return nil, err
379: 	}
380: 
381: 	keyPopFn := func(cn *container) (*kms.Keys, error) {
382: 		return c.keys, nil
383: 	}
384: 	cc, err := openContainer(ctx, channelContainer, con, keyPopFn)
385: 	if err != nil {
386: 		return nil, err
387: 	}
388: 
389: 	// Load and verify channel metadata
390: 	sha256Reader, err := crypto.NewSha256SumReader(ctx, cc.metaFile)
391: 	if err != nil {
392: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
393: 	}
394: 	sm, err := decodeChannelRecordingMeta(ctx, sha256Reader)
395: 	if err != nil {
396: 		return nil, err
397: 	}
398: 	err = cc.verifyMetadata(ctx, sha256Reader)
399: 	if err != nil {
400: 		return nil, err
401: 	}
402: 
403: 	channel := &Channel{
404: 		container: cc,
405: 		Meta:      sm,
406: 	}
407: 
408: 	return channel, nil
409: }
410: 
411: // NewMessagesWriter creates a writer for recording channel messages.
412: func (c *Connection) NewMessagesWriter(ctx context.Context, dir Direction) (io.Writer, error) {
413: 	const op = "bsr.(Connection).NewMessagesWriter"
414: 
415: 	switch {
416: 	case !ValidDirection(dir):
417: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: invalid direction: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
418: 	}
419: 
420: 	messagesName := fmt.Sprintf(messagesFile, dir.String())
421: 	_, err := c.container.WriteMeta(ctx, "messages", dir.String())
422: 	if err != nil {
423: 		return nil, err
424: 	}
425: 	m, err := c.container.create(ctx, messagesName)
426: 	if err != nil {
427: 		return nil, err
428: 	}
429: 
430: 	return checksum.NewFile(ctx, m, c.checksums)
431: }
432: 
433: // NewRequestsWriter creates a writer for recording connection requests.
434: func (c *Connection) NewRequestsWriter(ctx context.Context, dir Direction) (io.Writer, error) {
435: 	const op = "bsr.(Connection).NewRequestsWriter"
436: 
437: 	switch {
438: 	case !ValidDirection(dir):
439: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: invalid direction: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
440: 	}
441: 
442: 	requestName := fmt.Sprintf(requestsFile, dir.String())
443: 	_, err := c.container.WriteMeta(ctx, "requests", dir.String())
444: 	if err != nil {
445: 		return nil, err
446: 	}
447: 	m, err := c.container.create(ctx, requestName)
448: 	if err != nil {
449: 		return nil, err
450: 	}
451: 
452: 	return checksum.NewFile(ctx, m, c.checksums)
453: }
454: 
455: // Close closes the Connection container.
456: func (c *Connection) Close(ctx context.Context) error {
457: 	return c.container.close(ctx)
458: }
459: 
460: // Channel is a container in a bsr for a specific channel in a session
461: // container. It contains the files for the recorded channel.
462: type Channel struct {
463: 	*container
464: 
465: 	Meta *ChannelRecordingMeta
466: }
467: 
468: // Close closes the Channel container.
469: func (c *Channel) Close(ctx context.Context) error {
470: 	return c.container.close(ctx)
471: }
472: 
473: // NewMessagesWriter creates a writer for recording channel messages.
474: func (c *Channel) NewMessagesWriter(ctx context.Context, dir Direction) (io.Writer, error) {
475: 	const op = "bsr.(Channel).NewMessagesWriter"
476: 
477: 	switch {
478: 	case !ValidDirection(dir):
479: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: invalid direction: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
480: 	}
481: 
482: 	messagesName := fmt.Sprintf(messagesFile, dir.String())
483: 	_, err := c.container.WriteMeta(ctx, "messages", dir.String())
484: 	if err != nil {
485: 		return nil, err
486: 	}
487: 	m, err := c.container.create(ctx, messagesName)
488: 	if err != nil {
489: 		return nil, err
490: 	}
491: 
492: 	return checksum.NewFile(ctx, m, c.checksums)
493: }
494: 
495: // NewRequestsWriter creates a writer for recording channel requests.
496: func (c *Channel) NewRequestsWriter(ctx context.Context, dir Direction) (io.Writer, error) {
497: 	const op = "bsr.(Channel).NewRequestsWriter"
498: 
499: 	switch {
500: 	case !ValidDirection(dir):
501: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: invalid direction: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
502: 	}
503: 
504: 	requestName := fmt.Sprintf(requestsFile, dir.String())
505: 	_, err := c.container.WriteMeta(ctx, "requests", dir.String())
506: 	if err != nil {
507: 		return nil, err
508: 	}
509: 	m, err := c.container.create(ctx, requestName)
510: 	if err != nil {
511: 		return nil, err
512: 	}
513: 
514: 	return checksum.NewFile(ctx, m, c.checksums)
515: }
516: 
517: // OpenMessageScanner opens a ChunkScanner for a channel's recorded messages.
518: func (c *Channel) OpenMessageScanner(ctx context.Context, dir Direction) (*ChunkScanner, error) {
519: 	const op = "bsr.(Channel).OpenMessageScanner"
520: 
521: 	messagesName := fmt.Sprintf(messagesFile, dir.String())
522: 	m, err := c.container.container.OpenFile(ctx, messagesName, storage.WithFileAccessMode(storage.ReadOnly))
523: 	if err != nil {
524: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
525: 	}
526: 	expectedSum, err := c.shaSums.Sum(messagesName)
527: 	if err != nil {
528: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
529: 	}
530: 	return NewChunkScanner(ctx, m, WithSha256Sum(expectedSum))
531: }
532: 
533: // OpenRequestScanner opens a ChunkScanner for a channel's recorded requests.
534: func (c *Channel) OpenRequestScanner(ctx context.Context, dir Direction) (*ChunkScanner, error) {
535: 	const op = "bsr.(Channel).OpenRequestScanner"
536: 
537: 	requestName := fmt.Sprintf(requestsFile, dir.String())
538: 	m, err := c.container.container.OpenFile(ctx, requestName, storage.WithFileAccessMode(storage.ReadOnly))
539: 	if err != nil {
540: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
541: 	}

543: 	if err != nil {
544: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", op, err)
545: 	}
546: 	return NewChunkScanner(ctx, m, WithSha256Sum(expectedSum))
547: }
